name: H3-py (Uber H3 Hexagonal Geospatial Indexing)

performance.notes: C extension; very fast indexing; billions of cells per second possible

agent_readiness.notes: Hierarchical hexagonal grid indexing for geospatial analysis. import h3. h = h3.latlng_to_cell(37.775, -122.418, resolution=9). h3.cell_to_latlng(h). neighbors = h3.grid_disk(h, k=1). compact = h3.compact_cells(cells). h3.cell_to_parent(h, resolution=7). h3.cells_to_h3shape(cells). Resolution 0-15 (0=coarsest ~4M km2, 15=finest ~0.9m2). Great for: spatial aggregation, rideshare routing, supply-demand matching. H3 4.x API renamed from H3 3.x (latlng_to_cell vs geo_to_h3).

gotchas: H3 4.x renamed functions from 3.x — breaking change (latlng_to_cell vs geo_to_h3)
Hexagonal cells are not square — don't assume equal area at all resolutions
Polygon-to-cells fills interior — use h3.polygon_to_cells carefully for complex shapes
best_when: Spatial aggregation and binning at consistent cell sizes
Rideshare/delivery demand mapping and supply-demand balancing
Spatial joins at scale where hexagonal grid outperforms traditional approaches
Hierarchical spatial analysis across multiple resolutions
avoid_when: When rectangular grid indexing is needed (use S2 or QuadTree)
When traditional lat/lon grid is sufficient
summary: H3-py provides Uber's hexagonal geospatial indexing. h3.latlng_to_cell(lat, lng, resolution=9). h3.grid_disk(h, k=1). h3.compact_cells(cells). 16 resolutions (0=continent, 15=sub-meter). C extension, very fast. Apache-2.0 by Uber. Best for spatial aggregation, demand mapping, and hierarchical spatial analysis. H3 4.x renamed functions from 3.x — check version compatibility.
